what_it_does: Defines a single radial-view BINDING dict named 'by_lens' whose sectors are the rows of the `projections` registry (corpus lenses like topics/principles/worldview/repo/history), and a PROJECTION_SPACE_META dict mapping each projection-space id to operator-facing {name, is} human meaning.
observations: BINDING has id, label, meta {name,is,fills,why}, angle_from='projections', radius_from='time', order_by='count'.
order_by='count' is explicitly called the 'HONEST fallback' because projection rows have no inter-row edges to order by.
PROJECTION_SPACE_META covers common_knowledge, history, principles, topics, worldview, repo, operators, what, claimed_status, and '—' (Unfiled).

Comments explain the meta lives here (not in projections/_meta.py) because runtime/ on sys.path makes `import projections` resolve to runtime/projections.py, so a projections._meta import would silently fail.
'operators' deliberately displays as 'Roles' so a human operator doesn't read it as being about themselves (name != id).
Header comment claims registry-is-truth: dropping a projections/<id>.py makes it appear as a sector with no code edit.
standouts: Honest-fallback rationale for order_by='count' — refuses to fake a sequence where no inter-row edges exist.
Collision avoidance: meta kept in bindings/ because `import projections` resolves to the runtime module, not the registry folder.
conventions: Follows the {id: {name, is}} legibility-type shared with kinds/raw.py, nodes/_meta.py, bindings/grouped.py.
Operator-law: translates internal terms to operator language, never exposing 'lens/embed/vector space'.
Marks human-meaning entries TENTATIVE pending Tim/DNA ratification.
notes: A registry-driven radial view binding whose sectors are the corpus projection-spaces, plus the operator-facing names for those spaces.
questions: Where is the EVENT->ROW edge (corpus.record carrying `projection`) actually consumed to map events to sectors?
What is the SEED §95 growth front referenced for edges-between-registry-rows?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the file states it renders the lenses themselves as a registry-true view and it provides exactly that binding plus the sector meta.
apparent_themes: epistemic honesty (no faked ordering, no inferred status)
intention_signals: Trying to make 'the ways of looking' themselves a navigable view that automatically reflects whatever projection-spaces exist, while keeping the human-facing labels honest and operator-readable.
novelty: The explicit 'honest fallback' ordering and the import-collision workaround documented inline are distinctive.
contribution: Contributes a registry-driven binding that turns corpus projection-spaces into a navigable sector view, plus the operator-language name/description for each projection-space and the '—' unfiled remainder.
summary_for_embedding: A radial-view binding 'by_lens' whose sectors are the rows of the projections registry, paired with operator-facing name/description meta for each projection-space.
intention_for_embedding: To let an operator browse the set of corpus lenses (ways of looking) as a registry-true, honestly-ordered, plainly-labeled view.
